meals.morning: Warm lemon water + 7 soaked almonds + 3 walnuts + 1 tsp chia seeds

breakfast.name: Moong Dal Chilla + Paneer
steps: Soak moong dal in water for 8 hours or overnight
Drain and grind into smooth batter with little water
Add salt and chopped green chilli to the batter
Heat non-stick pan on medium and lightly grease with oil
Pour batter and spread in a circular motion like dosa
Cook on medium heat until edges lift, then flip
Cook other side until golden brown (about 1–2 minutes)
Add grated paneer as stuffing and fold in half
Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup
prep: Soak 1 tsp chia seeds in water overnight

lunch.name: Dal + Roti + Salad
steps: Wash dal 2–3 times until water runs clear
Add dal to pressure cooker with 2.5 cups water, pinch of turmeric and salt
Pressure cook for 3 whistles on medium flame
Heat 1 tsp oil in a tadka pan, add jeera and let it splutter
Add finely chopped garlic and sauté until light golden
Pour the tadka into the cooked dal and mix well
Simmer on low for 5–7 minutes, adjust salt and consistency
Prepare fresh salad: slice cucumber, carrot, onion, and tomato
Squeeze lemon and sprinkle chaat masala on the salad
Serve dal hot with fresh roti and salad on the side

snack.name: Roasted Chana + Pumpkin Seeds
steps: Take roasted chana in a bowl
Add pumpkin seeds and mix well
Optionally sprinkle a pinch of chaat masala and black salt
Consume as evening snack with a cup of green tea

dinner.name: Paneer Bhurji (Low Oil)
steps: Crumble the paneer into small pieces and keep aside
Heat 1 tsp oil in a non-stick pan on medium flame
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent (3–4 minutes)
Add chopped tomato and cook until soft and mushy
Add turmeric (¼ tsp), red chilli powder (¼ tsp), and salt to taste
Mix well and cook the masala for 1–2 minutes
Add crumbled paneer and toss gently for 2–3 minutes on medium heat
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves
Serve hot as is or with 1 roti

meals.morning: Jeera water + 7 soaked almonds + 3 walnuts + 1 tsp chia seeds

breakfast.name: Vegetable Oats
steps: Dry roast oats in a pan on low heat for 2–3 minutes until lightly fragrant
Remove oats and set aside
Heat 1 tsp oil in the same pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter
Add finely chopped onion and sauté for 2 minutes
Add diced mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, peas, capsicum) and sauté for 3–4 minutes
Add 2 cups water, salt, and a pinch of turmeric; bring to a boil
Add the roasted oats and stir well
Cook on low heat for 3–4 minutes until thick and creamy
Garnish with coriander leaves and a squeeze of lemon
Serve hot
prep: Soak 1 tsp chia seeds in water overnight

lunch.name: Rajma + Rice
steps: Soak rajma in water overnight (at least 8 hours)
Drain and pressure cook rajma with fresh water and salt (5–6 whistles)
Heat 1 tsp oil in a heavy-bottomed pan
Add cumin seeds and let them splutter
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and oil separates
Add ginger-garlic paste (1 tsp) and cook for 1 minute
Add turmeric (¼ tsp), red chilli powder (½ tsp), coriander powder (1 tsp), and garam masala (½ tsp)
Add the cooked rajma with its water and mix well
Simmer on low heat for 10–15 minutes until gravy thickens
Mash a few rajma with the back of a spoon for creamy texture
Cook rice separately: wash and boil with 2 cups water until fluffy
Serve rajma hot over steamed rice, garnished with coriander

snack.name: Paneer + Flax Seeds
steps: Cut paneer into small cubes or slices
Dry roast flax seeds in a pan on low heat for 1–2 minutes until fragrant
Optionally sprinkle a pinch of black pepper and chaat masala on paneer
Eat paneer cubes with roasted flax seeds as a protein-rich snack

dinner.name: Tofu Stir Fry
steps: Press tofu with a kitchen towel to remove excess moisture
Cut tofu into bite-sized cubes
Heat 1 tsp oil in a non-stick pan or wok on high flame
Add tofu cubes in a single layer and sear for 2–3 minutes per side until golden
Remove tofu and keep aside
In the same pan, add sliced bell peppers, broccoli florets, and snap peas
Stir fry vegetables on high heat for 3–4 minutes (keep them crunchy)
Add soy sauce (1 tsp), black pepper, and a pinch of salt
Return the tofu to the pan and toss everything together for 1 minute
Serve hot, optionally with a squeeze of lemon

meals.morning: Warm water + 7 soaked almonds + 3 walnuts + 1 tsp chia seeds

breakfast.name: Sprouts Bowl (Curd Optional)
steps: Rinse sprouted moong thoroughly under running water
Boil sprouts in water for 5–7 minutes if you prefer them soft, or use raw for crunch
Drain and transfer to a bowl
Add finely chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli
Squeeze half a lemon and add salt to taste
Add chaat masala (½ tsp) and mix well
Top with curd if preferred, or skip for a lighter version
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve immediately
prep: Soak 1 tsp chia seeds in water overnight

lunch.name: Chole + Roti
steps: Soak chole (kabuli chana) in water overnight with a pinch of baking soda
Drain, rinse, and pressure cook with fresh water, salt, and 1 tea bag for dark colour (5–6 whistles)
Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and bay leaf
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until deep golden
Add ginger-garlic paste (1 tsp) and cook for 1 minute
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until oil separates
Add chole masala (1 tsp), turmeric (¼ tsp), red chilli powder (½ tsp), and amchur (½ tsp)
Add the cooked chole with some of its water, mix well
Simmer on low heat for 15 minutes, mashing a few chole for thickness
Garnish with fresh coriander and ginger julienne
Serve hot with fresh roti

snack.name: Corn + Peanuts + Sunflower Seeds
steps: Boil sweet corn kernels in water with salt for 8–10 minutes until tender
Drain and transfer to a bowl
Dry roast peanuts in a pan for 2–3 minutes on low heat
Add sunflower seeds and roast for another minute
Mix everything together in the bowl
Add salt, black pepper, lemon juice, and chaat masala to taste
Toss well and serve as a warm or room-temperature snack

dinner.name: Moong Dal Soup
steps: Wash moong dal 2–3 times and soak for 30 minutes
Pressure cook dal with 2.5 cups water, turmeric (¼ tsp), and salt (2–3 whistles)
Let pressure release, then mash or blend the dal lightly for a soupy consistency
For the tadka: heat ½ tsp ghee, add finely chopped garlic and let it turn golden
Add cumin seeds (½ tsp), a pinch of hing, and dried red chilli
Pour the tadka into the dal soup and stir well
Add a squeeze of lemon juice and adjust salt
Simmer on low for 3–4 minutes
Serve warm in a bowl, garnished with fresh coriander

meals.morning: Jeera water + 7 soaked almonds + 3 walnuts + 1 tsp chia seeds

breakfast.name: Besan Chilla
steps: Take besan in a bowl and gradually add water to make a smooth, pourable batter (like dosa batter consistency)
Add finely chopped onion, tomato, green chilli, and coriander to the batter
Add salt, turmeric (¼ tsp), red chilli powder (¼ tsp), and ajwain (½ tsp)
Mix well and let the batter rest for 5 minutes
Heat a non-stick pan on medium flame and lightly grease with ½ tsp oil
Pour a ladleful of batter and spread in a circular motion
Drizzle a few drops of oil around the edges
Cook on medium heat until the bottom is golden and edges lift (2–3 minutes)
Flip carefully and cook the other side for 1–2 minutes
Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup
prep: Soak 1 tsp chia seeds in water overnight

lunch.name: Paneer Curry + Roti
steps: Cut paneer into cubes and soak in warm water for 5 minutes to soften
Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them splutter
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown (4–5 minutes)
Add ginger-garlic paste (1 tsp) and cook for 1 minute
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and cook until soft and oil separates
Add turmeric (¼ tsp), red chilli powder (½ tsp), coriander powder (1 tsp), and garam masala (½ tsp)
Cook the masala for 2 minutes until fragrant
Add ½ cup water and bring to a simmer
Drain the paneer and add cubes to the gravy
Simmer on low for 5 minutes, do not stir too much to keep paneer intact
Garnish with coriander and serve hot with fresh roti

snack.name: Sprouts + Pumpkin Seeds
steps: Rinse sprouts and lightly steam or boil for 5 minutes if preferred soft
Drain and transfer to a bowl
Add pumpkin seeds and mix gently
Season with salt, lemon juice, and a pinch of chaat masala
Serve immediately as an evening protein snack

dinner.name: Vegetable Khichdi
steps: Wash rice and moong dal together 2–3 times until water runs clear
Soak for 15–20 minutes, then drain
Heat ½ tsp ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds, and let them splutter
Add a pinch of hing, turmeric (¼ tsp), and finely chopped ginger
Add diced vegetables (carrot, beans, peas, potato) and sauté for 2 minutes
Add the soaked rice-dal mixture and stir gently
Add 2.5 cups water, salt to taste, and mix well
Close the lid and pressure cook for 3 whistles on medium flame
Let pressure release naturally
Mash gently with a spoon for a creamy texture
Serve warm with a dollop of ghee and pickle on the side

meals.morning: Warm water + 7 soaked almonds + 3 walnuts + 1 tsp chia seeds

breakfast.name: Upma + Peanuts
steps: Dry roast suji (rava) in a pan on low heat for 3–4 minutes until lightly golden and fragrant
Remove and set aside
In the same pan, heat 1 tsp oil and add mustard seeds, urad dal (½ tsp), and curry leaves
Add peanuts and roast for 1 minute until light brown
Add finely chopped onion and sauté for 2 minutes
Add diced carrot, peas, and beans; sauté for 2–3 minutes
Add 2 cups water, salt, and a pinch of turmeric; bring to a rolling boil
Reduce heat to low and slowly add the roasted suji while stirring continuously to avoid lumps
Cook on low heat for 2–3 minutes until thick and all water is absorbed
Cover and let it rest for 2 minutes
Garnish with fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon; serve hot
prep: Soak 1 tsp chia seeds in water overnight

lunch.name: Tofu Curry + Rice
steps: Press tofu to remove excess water, then cut into cubes
Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them splutter
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until golden (3–4 minutes)
Add ginger-garlic paste (1 tsp) and cook for 1 minute
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy
Add turmeric (¼ tsp), red chilli powder (½ tsp), coriander powder (1 tsp), and salt
Cook the masala for 2 minutes until oil separates
Add tofu cubes gently and coat with the masala
Add ½ cup water, cover, and simmer for 8–10 minutes on low heat
For rice: wash and cook 1 cup rice with 2 cups water until fluffy
Garnish curry with coriander and serve hot with steamed rice

snack.name: Roasted Makhana
steps: Heat a heavy-bottomed pan or kadai on low flame
Add makhana (fox nuts) to the dry pan — no oil needed
Roast on low heat, stirring continuously for 5–7 minutes
They will become crispy and crunchy when done
Remove from heat and sprinkle salt and black pepper to taste
Optionally add a pinch of chaat masala for extra flavour
Let them cool for a minute and enjoy as a light crunchy snack

dinner.name: Dal + Sabzi + Roti
steps: Wash toor or masoor dal 2–3 times and pressure cook with 2.5 cups water, turmeric, and salt (3 whistles)
For tadka: heat ½ tsp ghee, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, and dried red chilli
Add chopped garlic (2 cloves) and sauté until golden
Pour tadka into the cooked dal, mix, and simmer for 5 minutes
For sabzi: heat ½ tsp oil, add cumin, and any seasonal vegetable (lauki, tori, or bhindi)
Add salt, turmeric, and cook covered on low heat until tender (8–10 minutes)
Serve hot dal and sabzi with fresh roti

meals.morning: Warm lemon water + 7 soaked almonds + 3 walnuts + 1 tsp chia seeds

breakfast.name: Oats + Milk + Seeds + Fruit
steps: Pour milk into a saucepan and bring to a gentle simmer on medium heat
Add rolled oats and stir well
Cook on low heat for 3–4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and creamy
Transfer to a bowl and let it cool slightly
Chop a banana or any seasonal fruit (apple, mango, papaya) into small pieces
Top the oats with chopped fruit
Sprinkle watermelon seeds and a drizzle of honey if desired
Serve warm or at room temperature
prep: Soak 1 tsp chia seeds in water overnight

lunch.name: Rajma + Roti
steps: Soak rajma overnight (at least 8 hours) in plenty of water
Drain and pressure cook with fresh water and salt (5–6 whistles)
Heat ½ tsp oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown
Add ginger-garlic paste (1 tsp) and cook for 1 minute
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until oil separates
Add rajma masala (1 tsp), turmeric, red chilli powder, and salt
Add cooked rajma with its water and simmer on low for 10–15 minutes
Mash a few beans with the back of a spoon for a thicker gravy
Garnish with coriander and serve hot with roti

snack.name: Paneer + Seeds
steps: Cut paneer into bite-sized cubes
Lightly roast watermelon seeds in a dry pan for 1–2 minutes on low heat
Optionally sprinkle black pepper and a pinch of salt on paneer cubes
Eat together as a high-protein evening snack

dinner.name: Tofu Stir Fry
steps: Press tofu between kitchen towels for 10 minutes to remove moisture
Cut tofu into bite-sized cubes
Heat 1 tsp oil in a wok or non-stick pan on high flame
Add tofu cubes and sear for 2–3 minutes on each side until golden and crispy
Remove tofu and set aside
In the same pan, add sliced onion, bell pepper, and any available vegetables
Stir fry on high heat for 3–4 minutes, keeping vegetables crunchy
Add soy sauce (1 tsp), black pepper, and a pinch of salt
Return the tofu to the pan and toss everything together for 1 minute
Serve hot, garnished with sesame seeds and spring onion if available

meals.morning: Warm water + 7 soaked almonds + 3 walnuts + 1 tsp chia seeds

breakfast.name: Sprouts + Fruit
steps: Rinse sprouted moong well under running water
Lightly boil or steam for 5 minutes if you prefer them soft
Drain and transfer to a bowl
Chop seasonal fruit (apple, pomegranate, or banana) into small pieces
Add fruit to the sprouts and toss gently
Season with salt, lemon juice, and chaat masala
Mix well and serve immediately as a fresh, light breakfast
prep: Soak 1 tsp chia seeds in water overnight

lunch.name: Chole + Rice
steps: Soak chole overnight in water with a pinch of baking soda
Drain, rinse, and pressure cook with fresh water, salt, and 1 tea bag (5–6 whistles)
Heat 1 tsp oil, add cumin seeds and bay leaf
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until golden
Add ginger-garlic paste (1 tsp) and cook for 1 minute
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and oil separates
Add chole masala (1 tsp), turmeric, red chilli powder, and amchur
Add the cooked chole with water and simmer for 15 minutes on low
Mash a few chole for thicker gravy and adjust salt
Cook rice separately: wash and boil with 2 cups water until fluffy
Serve chole hot over rice, garnished with coriander and ginger julienne

snack.name: Chana + Seeds
steps: Take roasted chana (black or white) in a bowl
Add mixed seeds (pumpkin, sunflower, flax) and toss together
Sprinkle a pinch of salt, black pepper, and chaat masala
Squeeze a few drops of lemon for freshness
Mix well and enjoy as a crunchy, protein-rich snack

dinner.name: Light Dal + Salad
steps: Wash masoor or moong dal 2–3 times until water runs clear
Pressure cook with 2.5 cups water, turmeric (¼ tsp), and salt (3 whistles)
For tadka: heat ½ tsp ghee, add cumin seeds, chopped garlic (2 cloves), and dried red chilli
Once garlic is golden, pour the tadka into the dal
Add a squeeze of lemon and simmer for 3–4 minutes
For salad: slice cucumber, carrot, onion, and tomato
Season salad with salt, lemon, and a pinch of chaat masala
Serve dal warm alongside the fresh salad — a light end to the week
